Title: Plastidial starch phosphorylase regulates maltodextrin turnover during starch granule initiation in Arabidopsis leaves

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutants (phs1-1, phs1-2); triple and quadruple mutant analysis with sbe2.1 sbe2.2 and ss4; zymogram enzyme assay; western blot; metabolite profiling; TEM/light microscopy; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS); Supplementary Data Set 1; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/buffer in maltodextrin turnover under disrupted starch synthesis backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes maltodextrin turnover, limits maltose and maltodextrin accumulation, supports formation of insoluble linear α-glucans, maintains chloroplast integrity and plant growth in sbe2.1 sbe2.2 background
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: transient carbon storage and carbon homeostasis in chloroplasts; supports normal growth
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod; end-of-light and end-of-dark sampling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: shorter α-glucans and glucose-1-phosphate in phosphorolysis; longer α-glucans in synthetic direction
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: transitory starch synthesis/initiation and maltodextrin turnover module
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: modulating PHS1 could help tune leaf transitory starch turnover and carbon buffering under perturbed starch biosynthesis, but effects are highly genetic-background dependent
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product; supplies/turns over maltodextrin pool connected to insoluble α-glucan formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PHS1 in the sbe2.1 sbe2.2 background caused additional accumulation of maltose and maltodextrins. 2. PHS1 deficiency nearly abolished insoluble linear α-glucans and worsened chloroplast defects and growth retardation. 3. Additional ss4 mutation partially rescued these phenotypes, supporting a central buffering role of PHS1 in chloroplast maltodextrin turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single phs1 mutant had little effect in WT background, indicating conditional importance revealed when starch branching is disrupted; authors propose both synthetic and degradative roles for PHS1.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reversibly transfers glucosyl units between α-1,4-glucans and glucose-1-phosphate, extending maltodextrins in the synthetic direction or phosphorolytically shortening them to release glucose-1-phosphate

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant (ss4); quadruple mutant analysis in sbe2.1 sbe2.2 phs1-1 and triple mutant analysis in sbe2.1 sbe2.2; western blot; zymogram; metabolite profiling; TEM/light microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS); Supplementary Data Set 1; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for starch granule initiation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes formation of short α-glucan primers and soluble maltodextrin pool; loss of SS4 strongly decreases maltose and maltodextrin accumulation in sbe2.1 sbe2.2 phs1-1 and partially rescues growth/chloroplast integrity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: transient starch initiation and carbon partitioning in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod; end-of-light and end-of-dark sampling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: starch granule initiation module of transitory starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SS4 manipulation strongly impacts starch initiation and maltodextrin homeostasis; useful for engineering granule initiation traits but may affect growth depending on background
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; forms immediate primers for target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS4 activity/protein increased in sbe2.1 sbe2.2 phs1-1. 2. Loss of SS4 in the triple-mutant background sharply reduced maltose and maltodextrins and restored insoluble linear α-glucan formation and growth. 3. SS4 therefore promotes primer formation feeding the maltodextrin pool and strongly influences starch granule initiation context.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously considered essential for starch initiation, loss of SS4 in this perturbed background unexpectedly ameliorated the phs1-associated defects, highlighting context dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers glucosyl residues from ADP-glucose to α-1,4-glucan chains, producing short linear glucan primers used for starch granule initiation

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function double mutant background analysis with sbe2.1 sbe2.2 combined with phs1 and ss4 mutations; starch staining; metabolite profiling; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS); Supplementary Data Set 1; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: branch-switch enzyme for starch granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: absence contributes to failure of normal starch granule formation and accumulation of soluble maltose/maltodextrins and insoluble linear α-glucans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: starch architecture and transient carbon storage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: engineering SBE2.1 affects starch granule architecture and soluble glucan overflow, but severe pleiotropic growth effects are likely when branching is strongly reduced
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms branched structure required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBE2.1 acts with SBE2.2 to enable normal starch granule formation. 2. In the double-mutant background, loss of PHS1 or SS4 revealed altered maltodextrin turnover and insoluble linear α-glucan formation. 3. SBE2.1 is a foundational pathway component of the genetic system studied.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primarily provides the mutant background enabling discovery of PHS1 function; still central to the reported metabolite phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces α-1,6 branch linkages into elongating α-1,4-glucan chains during starch biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function double mutant background analysis with sbe2.1 sbe2.2 combined with phs1 and ss4 mutations; starch staining; metabolite profiling; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS); Supplementary Data Set 1; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: branch-switch enzyme for starch granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: absence contributes to failure of normal starch granule formation and accumulation of soluble maltose/maltodextrins and insoluble linear α-glucans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: starch architecture and transient carbon storage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: engineering SBE2.2 affects starch granule architecture and soluble glucan overflow, but strong pleiotropic effects are likely in combination with SBE2.1 loss
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms branched structure required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBE2.2 acts with SBE2.1 to enable normal starch granule formation. 2. In the double-mutant background, loss of PHS1 or SS4 revealed altered maltodextrin turnover and insoluble linear α-glucan formation. 3. SBE2.2 is a foundational pathway component of the genetic system studied.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primarily provides the mutant background enabling discovery of PHS1 function; still central to the reported metabolite phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces α-1,6 branch linkages into elongating α-1,4-glucan chains during starch biosynthesis
